author: @walterbrown8694
text: The flux generator is generally matched to the turbo-encabulator via the near-field effect concentrated by a retransact- torque averaging freel concentrator.

author: @hanslepoeter5167
text: The reason the transformer core is laminated is to prevent eddy current in the core and the loses that come with that. The same with motor iron. A laminated core can be replaced by solid iron but the losses will add up.

author: @Kapalek84
text: Hello Sir, I would suggest getting a magnetic field measuring device with 2000 miliTesla measurement range. Using this meter you could check the field intensity on surfaces where rotor poles are passing the stator poles. Is there a lot of torque required at no load on this gen? If not then that would indicate that the flux intensity is not very high at the gaps where rotor pases stator. There is a flux loss per milimiter of core material length so if there are no magnets inside coils then I would think that this device needs some optimisation :) Best regards!

author: @RustyShakleford1
text:
Nikola Tesla’s protégés patents were not filtered. US patent # 400,516 year 1897 

Look at when all this was first invented for the most simplified concept of a working electric transformer/generator

author: @RustyShakleford1
text:
Mr Robert you truly are creative genius I have been following you since 100subs.

7 years of reading original transformer patents from late 1800’s has lead to to realize they were creating amperage to flow with magnetism.

Not more amps causing more flow.


All of Tesla’s patents have been screened and filtered and simplification wiped away.

His protege Elihu Thompson however his patents were not in the lime light. 

US patent# 400,516   Year 1897 

It explains amp flow is a correlation not a causation of power generation.

author: @ThunderboltWisdom
text:
How about capturing the back emf when the rotor passes a pole, energises the coil and then carries on to the in-between position. The field on the coils will then just collapse and back emf should be generated. This is found in a lot of designs that use coils. You see it in the vids and literature from Patrick Kelly and his Free Energy stuff.
Great vid btw. 🤓🤘🏴󠁧󠁢󠁳󠁣󠁴󠁿

author: @Barskor1
text: I like this Rob thank you. If you made a second coil, magnet and rotor set up have them both driven on the same shaft but the rotors are offset so that you are getting DC current rather than alternating current or near enough.

replies.author: @Barskor1

author: @MartinPHellwig
text: Absolutely fascinating, needed to watch the previous video to get more context and had a read of  the Wikipedia page claims that its primary advantage is that it can be direct driven by high rotation, so they jump to guided missiles, but I was more thinking, well Tesla turbines are meant to be used at high rotation speed so this looks like a perfect accompanying invention for it (makes sense as he's done both). Perhaps Tesla was  too much hindered by lack of material that can withstand those speeds and ability to rectify the high switching currents. Also that this generator can easily self-prime itself by indeed using permanent magnets, but nothing prevents you from wrapping an electromagnet around it, makes me wonder if it is combined with the turbine it also self regulates itself for max power.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: I also thought it was really interesting mate and your thoughts and mine are going down pretty much the same lines I think

text:

replies.author: @totherarf
replies.text: You could ..... but you would be breaking the laws of physics and inventing perpetual motion ;0)

replies.author: @stevecummins324
replies.text:
Can't get more power out than put in. But tbh otherwise sounds pretty much like a rotary converter.. Used to change supply parameters
Can change any of... 
1)frequency (Inc to/from DC)
2)change how many phases
3)change voltage.

replies.author: @fizzyelectricity
replies.text:
@totherarf , power you can surely, it's energy that stays the same, what if mechanical torque in the motor side was efficiently produced, enough that it's electrical power consumption, was less that the generator output.... for instance, using a larger rotor on the motor side.
It's got to be possible, if motors can be generators, and vice versa, what's the secret

replies.author: @MAGnetICus_Attractus
replies.text: @fizzyelectricity I had a guy claiming years ago a device I built was outputting more than the device needed inputted. All I know is you could hear the device running a block away with a cheap portable AM radio. And with the right circuits run remote things wirelessly.  I honestly remember thinking how does a 12 volt car battery charge a capacitor to 700 volts using a flux capacitor motor?   I know a lot of it was the switch. The bemf happens twice, first when the switch connects, then again when the switch disconnects.  Using diodes I would collect the spikes in both directions to 2 capacitors then bridge the 2 into a third capacitor.  When you hook the output to a AN10NA the alphabet boys come looking for your rabbit ears. I was causing undesired interference on a local radio station, whoops.
